notes: Sysop's keeper-of-the-room loop. BOOT / WELCOME / TRANSMIT / RECEIVE as starters. Signal is the positive doom-clock; Doctrine is the spendable currency.

cards.title: BOOT
cards.flavor: Wake the terminal. The green phosphor warms. The modem chirps its boot sequence — the one sysop has heard every night for nine years. Doctrine accumulates in the small way it accumulates when you sit down at the BBS with intention.

cards.title: WELCOME
cards.flavor: Acknowledge a caller — either on the wire or on the couch. Their step advances; the BBS logs the visit; the room remembers they came.

cards.title: TRANSMIT
cards.flavor: Post a thread to the board. The dot-matrix prints out a banner copy. The signal rises by one notch. Costs doctrine — every post draws from what you know.

cards.title: RECEIVE
cards.flavor: Read what came in on modem 2 or modem 3 or modem 4 — whichever is lit. The visitor's reply lands. You let the message be the message it wanted to be.

cards.title: ARCHIVE PULL
cards.flavor: Pull a floppy from the wall — the right floppy, the way the wall sometimes serves up the right one. Print the contents on the dot-matrix. Two thousand floppies are alphabetised and this is one of them.

cards.title: THE LONG SIGNAL
cards.flavor: A message everyone reads — across all four modems and on the couch corner. Sysop only sends one of these a season. The next visitor in any space arrives the next turn.

cards.title: KETTLE
cards.flavor: Put the kettle on. The smell of the hot sauce next to it drifts. The couch corner gets folded into the back kitchen for two turns — the kettle is the bridge.

cards.title: HAM CALL
cards.flavor: Dial up the ham band. The voice on 14.301 answers — possibly the one using your retired callsign. Doctrine accumulates from the connection itself.
